message:
fix(toc): use github-slugger for consistent heading ID generation

Replace the custom slugify function with github-slugger to ensure that
generated heading IDs exactly match those produced by rehype-slug in the
markdown rendering pipeline.

Problem:
- The table of contents links were sometimes broken because the custom
  slugify function didn't handle all edge cases the same way as rehype-slug
- Heading IDs containing special characters, inline code, links, or
  emphasis markers would generate different slugs

Solution:
- Add github-slugger (^2.0.0) as a dependency
- Use GithubSlugger class which maintains state for duplicate headings
  (e.g., "Introduction", "Introduction-1", "Introduction-2")
- Add normalizeHeadingText() to strip inline markdown before slugging:
  - Remove HTML tags
  - Extract alt text from markdown images
  - Replace markdown links with link text only
  - Strip inline code backticks (keeping content)
  - Remove emphasis markers (*_~)
  - Collapse whitespace

Additional improvements:
- Fix code fence detection to handle both backtick and tilde fences
- Track fence type to properly match opening and closing fences
- Handle optional trailing hashes on ATX headings

This ensures clicking a TOC link always scrolls to the correct heading.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
